title: Interrupt Service Routine Template
description: Standard template showing how to write a proper ISR with register saving
code:
ORG 0003H ; INT0 vector address
LJMP ISR_INT0 ; Jump to actual ISR

ORG 0030H ; After vector table
ISR_INT0:
 PUSH ACC ; Save Accumulator
 PUSH PSW ; Save Program Status Word
 PUSH B ; Save B register (if needed)
 
 ; --- ISR Code Here ---
 ; Your interrupt handling code
 
 POP B ; Restore B register
 POP PSW ; Restore PSW
 POP ACC ; Restore Accumulator
 RETI ; Return from interrupt
explanation: This template demonstrates proper ISR structure: save all modified registers at start, restore in reverse order at end, use RETI to return. The LJMP at vector allows ISR to be placed anywhere in memory.

title: 8051 C Interrupt Configuration
description: C code showing how to enable and configure interrupts
explanation: In C, use 'interrupt' keyword with interrupt number. For 8051: interrupt 0=INT0, 1=Timer0, 2=INT1, 3=Timer1, 4=Serial. SFRs accessed directly or via registers.h.

title: Nested Interrupt Example
description: Example showing how to allow higher priority interrupts during ISR
code:
; Main program sets up interrupts
SETB EA ; Enable all interrupts
SETB EX0 ; Enable INT0 (lower priority)
SETB EX1 ; Enable INT1 (higher priority)
; Assume IP: PX1=1, PX0=0 (INT1 higher priority)

; ISR for INT0 (lower priority)
ORG 0003H
ISR_INT0:
 PUSH ACC
 PUSH PSW
 
 ; Enable nested interrupts
 SETB EA ; Allow higher priority interrupts
 
 ; --- Critical code ---
 ; If INT1 occurs here, it will interrupt
 
 CLR EA ; Disable nesting for rest of ISR
 
 POP PSW
 POP ACC
 RETI
explanation: Nested interrupts work when: 1) EA is set inside ISR, 2) Higher priority interrupt arrives. When INT1 (higher) interrupts INT0 (lower), INT1 ISR completes first, then INT0 continues. Use carefully to avoid stack overflow.

title: Timer Interrupt with Counter
description: ISR that counts timer overflows to create longer delays
code:
; Timer0 in 16-bit mode, auto-reload
ORG 000BH ; Timer0 vector
LJMP ISR_T0

ORG 0030H
INIT_T0:
 MOV TMOD, #01H ; Timer0 mode 1 (16-bit)
 MOV TH0, #0FCH ; Initial value (for 1000 cycles)
 MOV TL0, #18H
 SETB EA ; Enable interrupts
 SETB ET0 ; Enable Timer0 interrupt
 SETB TR0 ; Start timer
 SJMP $

ISR_T0:
 PUSH ACC
 PUSH PSW
 
 ; Reload timer initial value
 MOV TH0, #0FCH
 MOV TL0, #18H
 
 ; Increment counter
 INC counter_low
 CJNE counter_low, #100, DONE
 MOV counter_low, #0
 INC counter_high
 
DONE:
 POP PSW
 POP ACC
 RETI
explanation: Timer overflow occurs every 1000 machine cycles (~1ms at 12MHz). ISR counts 100 overflows = 100ms delay. This demonstrates using interrupts for precise timing without CPU polling.
